title: Open Cloze: Word types (grammar and function words)
intro: Words that commonly fill gaps in Open Cloze tasks. One word per gap; spelling counts.

categories.definition: Express ability, permission, obligation, possibility, or habit (e.g. will, can, must).
categories.words: will, would, can, could, may, might, must, should, need, used to

categories.definition: Form questions, negatives, and tenses (be, have, do and their forms).
categories.words: do, does, did, have, has, had, am, is, are, was, were, been, being

categories.definition: Specify whether the noun is definite (the), indefinite (a/an), or unspecified (no article).
Specify quantity or scope (how much, how many, which ones, all, some, etc.).
categories.words: some, any, no, every, each, all, both, either, neither, much, many, few, little, a few, a little, more, most, less, another, other, such

categories.definition: Set expressions of two (or more) words that function as a single unit (e.g. in order to, such as).
categories.example: We need to act as soon as possible.
categories.words: such as, as well as, in order to, so that, as long as, as soon as, in spite of, due to, owing to, according to, instead of, rather than, even though, as if, as though

categories.definition: Introduce a contrast or opposition between ideas.
categories.words: although, though, however, nevertheless, yet, while, whereas, despite, in spite of

categories.definition: Introduce purpose (why someone does something) or a condition (if something is true).
categories.words: so that, in order to, to, if, unless, as long as, provided that

categories.definition: Introduce a reason (why something happens) or a result (what follows).
categories.example: I stayed home because I was tired.
categories.words: because, as, since, so, therefore, thus, hence

categories.definition: Show when one action happens relative to another, or sequence in time.
categories.words: when, while, as, as soon as, before, after, until, once

categories.definition: Conjunction (that), negation (not), degree (so, such), or place/time reference (there, here, then, now).
categories.words: that, not, so, such, there, here, then, now

categories.definition: Show position, direction, cause, manner, or relationship between elements.
categories.words: in, on, at, to, from, for, with, without, by, about, of, into, out, up, down, between, among, through, across, despite, like, as

categories.definition: Show when something happens (point in time, duration, or period).
categories.words: at, on, in, for, since, during, before, after, until, till, by, from, to
who, whom, whose, which, that, what, where, when, why, how
